senses.definition: a slender threadlike spore produced by certain fungi, distinguished from other spores produced by the same fungus
examples: Phomopsis produces scolecospores that can be observed under a microscope.
The lab identified scolecospores in the fungal culture.

senses.definition: a genus of insects in the family Scoliidae commonly known as scoliid wasps; a term used in entomology
examples: The researcher collected several specimens of scoleryng for the survey.
Scoleryng larvae are parasitoids of beetle larvae.

senses.definition: the scolex is the head end of a tapeworm, equipped with hooks or suckers used to attach to the host’s intestine
examples: The scolex anchors the tapeworm to the intestinal wall.
Microscopic examination revealed a scolex with four suckers.

senses.definition: an archaic or dialectal term historically used to refer to a spoon or scoop, or a loose, scooped-out appearance
examples: The old cookbook uses scoley to describe a small spoon.
The coat had scoley edges along the seam.

senses.definition: a genus within the family Scoliidae, commonly called scoliid wasps
examples: Scolia species are large hornets found in various regions.
The biologist studied the nesting behavior of Scolia.

senses.definition: plural of scolex; in parasitology, the anterior end of a tapeworm containing hooks or suckers that attaches to the host
examples: The stained preparations showed multiple scolices on the tapeworm.
Scolices are characteristic features used in identification.

senses.definition: relating to the family Scoliidae of wasps; of or pertaining to scoliid wasps
examples: The scoliid wasp genus contains several large, robust species.
Researchers studied scoliid wasp venom properties.

senses.definition: the biological family Scoliidae, comprising large wasps often known as scoliid wasps
examples: Scoliidae is a family of solitary wasps.
Many scoliiidae species hunt beetle larvae.

senses.definition: relating to sclioiography or the study/recording of scoliosis, often used in medical imaging contexts
examples: The patient underwent a scolioographic evaluation.
Scoliographic imaging helped plan the surgery.

senses.definition: an anatomical or pathological term referring to a curved or bent position of the body incorporating scoliosis elements
examples: Scoliokyposis is observed in certain spinal deformities.
The report mentions scolio-kyposis as a combined curvature diagnosis.

senses.definition: a tumor or swelling associated with the spine or scoliosis contexts, historically used in medical literature
examples: The rare term scolio­ma appeared in old case reports.
Modern texts prefer more specific terms for spinal tumors.

senses.definition: plural of scolio­ma; tumors or swellings related to scoliosis contexts
examples: The pathology notes several scolio­mas in the archive study.
Scoliomas are rarely discussed in modern curricula.

senses.definition: a medical instrument used to measure the degree of spinal curvature in scoliosis
examples: The doctor used a scolio meter to assess the curve.
A higher reading on the scolio meter indicates greater curvature.
collocations: measure scoliosis with a scolio meter

senses.definition: an anatomical term referring to a shield-like structure or protective plate in certain organisms; used in paleontology or biology contexts involving scoliosis discussions
examples: The fossil shows a pronounced scolion.
Researchers described a protective scolion in the specimen.

senses.definition: relating to deformities of the spine characterized by scoliosis and rachitic (rickets-related) changes
examples: The patient exhibited scoliorachitic features on radiographs.
Scoliorachitic changes can complicate management.

senses.definition: plural of scoliosis; several cases or types of lateral curvature of the spine
examples: The clinic reported multiple scolioses in the cohort.
Treatment plans vary for different scolioses.

senses.definition: a medical condition where the spine curves sideways in an abnormal shape, often forming an S or C shape
examples: Scoliosis can develop in childhood or adolescence.
Regular exercise may help manage mild scoliosis.

senses.definition: relating to scoliosis or showing curvature of the spine
examples: The patient has a scolio­tic spine.
Scoliotic curves were measured on imaging.

senses.definition: a theoretical or rarely used term referring to a tone or pitch associated with scoliosis contexts; not a standard common term
examples: The term scolio tone appears in a historical text on spinal deformities.

senses.definition: a mineral or fossil term, or a historical name; usage is extremely rare in contemporary English
examples: The mineral specimen was labeled scolite in the old catalog.

senses.definition: a dialect or archaic spelling of scallop, a marine mollusk with a fan-shaped shell
examples: The fisherman sold a bag of scollops from the bay.
Scollop shells glittered on the shore.

senses.definition: having a scalloped edge or being cut into a series of curved, tooth-like shapes
examples: The pastry was scolloped around the edge.
She wore a dress with scolloped lace trim.

senses.definition: one who scolls or cuts scalloped edges; an archaic or dialectal term
examples: The scolloper shaped the fabric with decorative edges.

senses.definition: the act of creating scalloped edges; decorative edging technique
examples: Scolloping was popular on vintage table linens.

senses.definition: plural of scallop, a marine mollusk with a fan-shaped shell; also a cooking term for the meat of scallops
examples: We fried several scollops for dinner.
Scollops are often served sautéed.
